title: Tangy Turkish Lamb Iskender
description: A sour and springy Turkish dish featuring lamb, served in a hot pot vessel, perfect for midnight cravings. It is made with galangal-kha and long-fermentation-dough, and is nut-free-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Slice the lamb shoulder into thin strips and season with salt and black pepper.
Grill the lamb strips in a hot skillet until browned, then set aside.
Roll out the long-fermentation-dough and cut into thin strips.
Deep fry the dough strips until crispy and golden brown.
Mix the tomato paste, galangal-kha, lemon juice, and yogurt in a bowl.
Add the grilled lamb strips to the bowl and toss to coat with the sauce.
Serve the lamb and sauce over the fried dough strips in a hot pot vessel.
Serve with pita bread on the side.
